title: Ladakh
lead: ==~59,146 sq km, ~2.74 lakh people== (2011) — Buddhists, Muslims and tribal communities — under direct Central administration via a ==Lieutenant Governor, with no elected assembly==. The two ==Autonomous Hill Development Councils== (Leh, Kargil; 1997 Act) are the only elected bodies, subordinate to the LG.

items.title: Sixth Schedule
bullets: Constitutional protection for ==tribal identity, land and culture==

items.title: Recruitment
bullets: Separate ==Public Service Commission== / job reservations for locals

items.title: Lok Sabha
bullets: More representation — currently ==one MP for the entire UT==

items: ==Arts 244(2) & 275(1)== + Sixth Schedule govern tribal areas in ==Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram== through Autonomous District Councils.

ADCs can: legislate on ==land, forests== (excl. reserved), waterways, jhum cultivation, village administration; run ==village courts== under customary law; ==levy taxes and royalties==; regulate money-lending and trading by non-tribals; manage schools, dispensaries, markets, ferries.
A ==constitutional shield over land, culture and identity== — far stronger than panchayat law or the LAHDC Act.
==Loss of ‘State Subject’ protection==: pre-2019, Art 35A barred outsiders from buying land — its abrogation stokes fear of ==demographic influx==
==No legislature, no voice==: every applicable law is made by Parliament or executive order
==Ecological fragility==: glaciers, pastures and wetlands tied to tribal land use
==Cultural survival==: Ladakhi, Balti, Purgi languages; Buddhist and Shia customs; pastoral practices
==Diluted Hill Councils==: funds, functions and functionaries squeezed by the LG's authority post-2019

items.title: Challenges to Extension
bullets: ==Security==: strategic border territory in a live conflict zone
==Precedent==: could trigger demands from other UTs (Andaman & Nicobar)
==Intra-Ladakh divide==: Kargil (Muslim-majority) vs Leh (Buddhist-majority) differ on the protection sought
Sixth Schedule was ==designed for the North-East== — extension needs a ==constitutional amendment==
==Scope ambiguity==: dividing powers between ADC, LG and Parliament is complex

items.title: Solutions
bullets: ==Short term==: statutory strengthening of Hill Councils (LAHDC Act amendment); domicile-based recruitment guarantees
==Constitutional==: extend the Sixth Schedule or create a ==Ladakh-specific schedule analogous to Art 371==
==Dialogue==: revive High-Powered Committee talks with a time-bound mandate, including civil society
